column1: # Upload via: POST /api/content/samples  (with topic_id and file)
# ── Grammar & Vocabulary — 2 marks — Fill in the blanks ──────────────────────

column5: By next year, he ___ (complete) his degree.
They ___ (not / submit) the report by the deadline.
She ___ (read) the book when the phone rang.
By the time the guests arrive, the host ___ (prepare) the meal.
She have been working here for five years.
column6: She has been working here for five years.

column5: He will has finished the project by Friday.
column6: He will have finished the project by Friday.

column5: They had been completed the task before the meeting started.
column6: They had completed the task before the meeting started.

column5: Convert to Future Perfect: She finishes the report before the deadline.
column6: She will have finished the report before the deadline.

column5: Convert to Past Perfect Continuous: He works on the project for three hours.
column6: He had been working on the project for three hours.

column5: Convert from active to passive: The team completed the project last week.
column6: The project was completed by the team last week.

column5: Rearrange: completed / the / team / project / by / had / Friday
column6: The team had completed the project by Friday.

column5: Rearrange: been / for / working / she / three / has / years / here
column6: She has been working here for three years.

column5: Write a short functional message requesting a meeting with your manager.
column6: Model: Dear [Manager Name], I would like to request a brief meeting at your convenience to discuss the progress of the [project name]. Please let me know a suitable time. Thank you. Regards, [Name]

column5: Write a short notice informing employees about a change in office timings.
column6: Model: NOTICE — Change in Office Timings — Please note that effective from [date], office hours will change to [new timings]. All employees are requested to follow the new schedule. For queries, contact HR. — Management, [Date]

column5: Write a short essay (150 words) on the importance of effective communication in the workplace.
column6: Model: Effective communication is the backbone of any successful organisation. It ensures that information is shared clearly and efficiently between team members, managers, and clients. When communication is clear, misunderstandings are avoided and productivity increases. In professional settings, both verbal and written communication skills are essential. Listening carefully, speaking confidently, and writing clearly are key competencies that every employee must develop. Poor communication, on the other hand, leads to confusion, delays, and conflict. By investing in communication skills training, organisations can build stronger teams and achieve their goals more efficiently. In conclusion, effective communication is not just a soft skill — it is a professional necessity.
